steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side
steps.content: The phrase before the blank—ending with “ruthenium”—is an independent clause. The phrase after the blank—beginning with “the alloy”—is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the comma-conjunction rule
steps.content: A comma and a coordinating conjunction join two independent clauses. Choice D provides both: the comma and “but,” which also expresses the contrast between the attempted improvement and the unsuitable result. The other choices lack either the comma, the conjunction, or both. That matches choice D — D is correct.
